title: VAT impact of transfer pricing adjustments - Deloitte
summary: VAT impact of transfer pricing adjustments Deloitte
ai_summary: This article from Deloitte discusses the often-overlooked VAT implications that arise from transfer pricing adjustments. It highlights the importance for multinational enterprises to consider the indirect tax consequences when making changes to intercompany pricing.

title: GRA deepens transfer pricing drive with ATAF, World Bank
summary: GRA deepens transfer pricing drive with ATAF, World Bank The Point.gm
ai_summary: The Ghana Revenue Authority (GRA) is intensifying its transfer pricing enforcement efforts through collaboration with the African Tax Administration Forum (ATAF) and the World Bank. This initiative aims to strengthen Ghana's capacity to address transfer pricing challenges and ensure fair taxation of multinational enterprises.

title: Navigating permanent establishment risk in a remote work era: the OECD’s 2025 Commentary and local approaches - KPMG
summary: Navigating permanent establishment risk in a remote work era: the OECD’s 2025 Commentary and local approaches KPMG
ai_summary: This KPMG article examines the evolving risks of permanent establishment (PE) in the context of remote work, referencing the anticipated 2025 OECD Commentary. It discusses how multinational enterprises can navigate these challenges by understanding both international guidance and various local country approaches.

title: ITAT Quashes TP Assessment Orders as Consequential Orders Were Passed Beyond Limitation Period
summary: ITAT Hyderabad held that assessment orders passed pursuant to earlier remand directions were barred by limitation under Section 153. The Tribunal set aside all consequential transfer pricing assessment orders dated 06.12.2024. The post ITAT Quashes TP Assessment Orders as Consequential Orders Were Passed Beyond Limitation Period appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Hyderabad quashed transfer pricing assessment orders issued on December 6, 2024, ruling that they were passed beyond the statutory limitation period under Section 153. The Tribunal consequently set aside all related consequential transfer pricing assessment orders due to this procedural lapse.

title: Purchase of MHADA Leasehold Tenement Can Attract Section 56(2)(x): ITAT Mumbai
summary: Mumbai ITAT held that Section 56(2)(x) applies to purchase of MHADA leasehold property rights despite reliance on Section 50C rulings. However, the Tribunal directed the AO to obtain a DVO valuation before recomputing the addition. The post Purchase of MHADA Leasehold Tenement Can Attract Section 56(2)(x): ITAT Mumbai appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Mumbai ITAT held that Section 56(2)(x) of the Income Tax Act is applicable to the purchase of MHADA leasehold property rights. The Tribunal directed the Assessing Officer to obtain a District Valuation Officer (DVO) report before recomputing any additions under this section.

title: ITAT Deletes Section 68 Addition on Forfeited Loans Already Offered to Tax – Double Taxation Not Permissible
summary: Delhi ITAT held that unsecured loans already forfeited and offered to tax in a subsequent assessment year cannot again be taxed under Section 68 in the year of receipt. The Tribunal ruled that such action would result in impermissible double taxation. The post ITAT Deletes Section 68 Addition on Forfeited Loans Already Offered to Tax – Double Taxation Not Permissible appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Delhi ITAT ruled that unsecured loans previously forfeited and already offered to tax in a subsequent assessment year cannot be taxed again under Section 68 in the year of receipt. The Tribunal emphasized that such re-taxation would lead to impermissible double taxation for the assessee.

title: For Section 56(2)(x) Stamp Duty Value on Allotment Date Must Be Considered: ITAT Mumbai
summary: ITAT Mumbai held that stamp duty valuation on the date of allotment should be considered where property consideration was fixed earlier and payments were made through banking channels before registration. The post For Section 56(2)(x) Stamp Duty Value on Allotment Date Must Be Considered: ITAT Mumbai appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Mumbai ITAT held that for the purpose of Section 56(2)(x), the stamp duty valuation on the date of allotment should be considered for property transactions. This applies specifically when the property consideration was fixed earlier and payments were made via banking channels prior to registration.

title: ITAT Reduces Section 271(1)(b) Penalty by treating Multiple Notice Defaults as One
summary: The ITAT Visakhapatnam reduced a penalty under Section 271(1)(b) from Rs.30,000 to Rs.10,000 after treating non-compliance with three notices under Section 142(1) as a single default. The Tribunal granted partial relief while holding that the assessee failed to establish reasonable cause for non-compliance. The post ITAT Reduces Section 271(1)(b) Penalty by treating Multiple Notice Defaults as One appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Visakhapatnam reduced a penalty under Section 271(1)(b) from Rs.30,000 to Rs.10,000, treating multiple defaults for non-compliance with Section 142(1) notices as a single default. While granting partial relief, the Tribunal noted the assessee failed to provide reasonable cause for the non-compliance.

title: EY talks tax: EY Tax.Tech™ 2026: Building the future of Tax - AI in tax - what's working now and what's next (May 6, 2026)
summary: In this episode, panelists focus on current applications of artificial intelligence (AI) in tax, providing practical examples of how AI assists with core tax processes, how today's capabilities differ meaningfully from those available even two years ago, and considerations for moving from pilot efforts to production.
ai_summary: This EY webcast from May 6, 2026, focuses on the current applications of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in various tax processes, providing practical examples of its use. Panelists discuss evolving AI capabilities and strategic considerations for transitioning from pilot projects to full production within the tax function.

title: Clarification of the terms “Director” and “Officer” under Article 36 of the UAE Corporate Tax Law - KPMG
summary: Clarification of the terms “Director” and “Officer” under Article 36 of the UAE Corporate Tax Law KPMG
ai_summary: KPMG provided a clarification on the terms "Director" and "Officer" as defined under Article 36 of the UAE Corporate Tax Law. This interpretation is crucial for understanding corporate governance and potentially defining key personnel involved in MNE functions for transfer pricing analysis.

title: ITAT Grants Reckitt Relief in Marketing Intangibles Transfer Pricing Dispute - Studycafe
summary: ITAT Grants Reckitt Relief in Marketing Intangibles Transfer Pricing Dispute Studycafe
ai_summary: The Indian Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) provided relief to Reckitt in a transfer pricing dispute concerning marketing intangibles. This ruling addresses the arm's length valuation and attribution of profits related to brand promotion expenses incurred by the Indian entity.

title: CPC Cannot Change AOP Status to Co-operative Society in ITR Processing: ITAT Ahmedabad
summary: ITAT Ahmedabad held that CPC cannot suo motu reclassify an apartment owners association as a co-operative society while processing returns under Section 143(1). The Tribunal deleted the Rs. 97,690 demand after finding the adjustment beyond the scope of prima facie processing. The post CPC Cannot Change AOP Status to Co-operative Society in ITR Processing: ITAT Ahmedabad appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Ahmedabad ruled that the CPC cannot suo motu change the status of an Association of Persons (AOP) to a co-operative society during income tax return processing under Section 143(1). The Tribunal deleted a demand of Rs. 97,690, deeming the adjustment outside the scope of prima facie processing.

title: India’s TP reset for the digital economy: safe harbour and APA reforms - International Tax Review
summary: India’s TP reset for the digital economy: safe harbour and APA reforms International Tax Review
ai_summary: This article examines India's comprehensive overhaul of its transfer pricing framework concerning the digital economy. It specifically addresses anticipated reforms to safe harbour provisions and the Advance Pricing Agreement (APA) program to align with the evolving digital landscape.

title: AO Cannot Apply Post-2018 “Look-Through” Valuation Retrospectively – ITAT Deletes Massive Section 56 Additions
summary: ITAT Mumbai deleted additions exceeding ₹10.57 crore made under section 56(2)(vii)(c) after finding that the Assessing Officer wrongly adopted an amended valuation approach retrospectively. The Tribunal upheld the CIT(A)’s deletion in entirety. The post AO Cannot Apply Post-2018 “Look-Through” Valuation Retrospectively – ITAT Deletes Massive Section 56 Additions appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Mumbai ruled against the retrospective application of a post-2018 'look-through' valuation approach for Section 56(2)(vii)(c) additions. The Tribunal deleted additions exceeding ₹10.57 crore, affirming that amended valuation methodologies cannot be applied retroactively.

title: ITAT Deletes Software Cost Mark-Up Addition Holding Pure Reimbursement Is Not Profit-Making Service
summary: The ITAT Delhi ruled that reimbursement of software costs to foreign AEs on a cost-to-cost basis could not be treated as a profit-generating intra-group service. The Tribunal deleted the transfer pricing adjustment after finding the benchmarking method adopted by the TPO unjustified. The post ITAT Deletes Software Cost Mark-Up Addition Holding Pure Reimbursement Is Not Profit-Making Service appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Delhi ruled that pure reimbursement of software costs to foreign Associated Enterprises on a cost-to-cost basis should not be subject to a transfer pricing mark-up. The Tribunal deleted the transfer pricing adjustment, deeming the TPO’s benchmarking method for such non-profit-making services unjustified.

title: DCF Valuation Cannot Be Rejected Using Hindsight – ITAT Deletes ₹36.54 Crore Section 56(2)(viib) Addition
summary: ITAT Mumbai ruled that replacing projected cash flows with actual profits while applying the DCF method is legally impermissible. The decision reaffirmed that DCF valuation is inherently based on future estimates and business expectations. The post DCF Valuation Cannot Be Rejected Using Hindsight – ITAT Deletes ₹36.54 Crore Section 56(2)(viib) Addition appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Mumbai ruled that using hindsight to reject a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) valuation by replacing projected cash flows with actual profits is impermissible. The Tribunal deleted ₹36.54 crore additions under Section 56(2)(viib), reaffirming that DCF is inherently based on future estimates.

title: ITAT rejected TPO’s recharacterisation of Boeing India as Full-Risk entity in technical services arrangement
summary: Transfer Pricing Officer (TPO) had wrongly recharacterised Boeing India Defense Private Limited as a full-risk service provider despite the Associated Enterprise (AE) assuming the entire contractual and operational risks relating to defence support services rendered to the Indian Air Force (IAF). The post ITAT rejected TPO’s recharacterisation of Boeing India as Full-Risk entity in technical services arrangement appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT rejected the Transfer Pricing Officer's recharacterisation of Boeing India Defense Private Limited as a full-risk entity in its technical services arrangement. The Tribunal found that the Associated Enterprise, not Boeing India, assumed all contractual and operational risks for defense support services.

title: HMRC’s transfer pricing windfall: one-off or new normal? - Macfarlanes
summary: HMRC’s transfer pricing windfall: one-off or new normal? Macfarlanes
ai_summary: This article from Macfarlanes discusses whether HMRC's recent success in transfer pricing cases represents a temporary "windfall" or a sustained increase in enforcement. It analyzes the implications for multinational enterprises operating in the UK regarding their transfer pricing policies and compliance.

title: International Controlled Transactions Schedule (ICTS): a new UK transfer pricing reporting requirement for accounting periods commencing on or after 1 January 2027 - Saffery
summary: International Controlled Transactions Schedule (ICTS): a new UK transfer pricing reporting requirement for accounting periods commencing on or after 1 January 2027 Saffery
ai_summary: The UK is introducing a new International Controlled Transactions Schedule (ICTS) as a transfer pricing reporting requirement for accounting periods commencing on or after January 1, 2027. This signifies a major update to transfer pricing documentation and compliance obligations for multinational enterprises in the UK.

title: Amagi gets Rs 18 crore tax adjustment notice, to appeal transfer pricing order - Storyboard18
summary: Amagi gets Rs 18 crore tax adjustment notice, to appeal transfer pricing order Storyboard18
ai_summary: Amagi, an Indian company, has received a tax adjustment notice of Rs 18 crore related to a transfer pricing order. The company intends to appeal this order, indicating the beginning of a tax dispute regarding its international transactions.

title: Agricultural Land Also Covered by Section 56(2)(x) – ITAT Upholds Addition Based on DVO Valuation
summary: The Tribunal upheld tax addition where agricultural land was acquired below stamp duty valuation and DVO-determined fair market value. It ruled that agricultural status of land does not exclude applicability of section 56(2)(x). The post Agricultural Land Also Covered by Section 56(2)(x) – ITAT Upholds Addition Based on DVO Valuation appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT upheld a tax addition under Section 56(2)(x) where agricultural land was acquired below its stamp duty valuation and fair market value determined by the Departmental Valuation Officer (DVO). The Tribunal clarified that the agricultural status of the land does not exempt it from the applicability of this section.

title: ITAT Mumbai Excludes MPS Ltd. as Comparable, Deletes ₹18.80 Crore TP Adjustment in ITES Case
summary: ITAT Mumbai held that a company engaged in publishing platforms, software solutions, and product development could not be compared with a limited-risk captive ITES service provider. Exclusion of the comparable eliminated the transfer pricing adjustment. The post ITAT Mumbai Excludes MPS Ltd. as Comparable, Deletes ₹18.80 Crore TP Adjustment in ITES Case appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Mumbai has deleted a ₹18.80 crore transfer pricing adjustment in an ITES case by excluding MPS Ltd. as a comparable company. The Tribunal ruled that a company involved in publishing platforms, software solutions, and product development cannot be compared to a limited-risk captive ITES service provider.

title: Transfer Pricing Addition Sent Back as DRP Failed to Properly Examine Functional Comparability
summary: Tribunal found the DRP’s order cryptic and lacking proper analysis on similarity of business activities between the assessee and selected comparables. Fresh examination was directed regarding comparability and ALP computation. The post Transfer Pricing Addition Sent Back as DRP Failed to Properly Examine Functional Comparability appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Tribunal has remanded a transfer pricing addition for fresh examination, finding that the Dispute Resolution Panel (DRP) failed to properly analyze functional comparability between the assessee and selected comparables. The DRP's order was deemed cryptic and lacking a proper examination of business activity similarities.

title: SC LTC Ruling Cannot Retrospectively Create TDS Default: ITAT Ahmedabad
summary: ITAT ruled that although LTC exemption involving foreign travel stood disallowed by SC, such judgment could not retrospectively impose TDS default liability for periods covered by judicial interim protection. The post SC LTC Ruling Cannot Retrospectively Create TDS Default: ITAT Ahmedabad appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Ahmedabad ruled that a Supreme Court decision disallowing LTC exemption for foreign travel cannot retrospectively impose TDS default liability. The Tribunal clarified that such a judgment does not apply to periods covered by judicial interim protection.

title: No Double Taxation Allowed Even if Expense Claim Found Non-Genuine: ITAT Mumbai
summary: ITAT Mumbai held that although foreign commission expenditure was non-genuine and liable for disallowance, amounts already written back and taxed in a subsequent year could not again be taxed through disallowance in earlier years. The post No Double Taxation Allowed Even if Expense Claim Found Non-Genuine: ITAT Mumbai appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Mumbai ruled that even if a foreign commission expenditure claim is found non-genuine and disallowable, amounts already written back and taxed in a subsequent year cannot be taxed again. This decision reinforces the principle against double taxation for the same income.

title: ITAT Allows BAPA Margin for Non-US AE Transactions Due to Identical FAR Profile
summary: The Tribunal ruled that margins agreed under a Bilateral Advance Pricing Agreement may be used for non-covered AEs when transactions are functionally similar. The decision emphasized consistency and the lack of separate benchmarking by the TPO. The post ITAT Allows BAPA Margin for Non-US AE Transactions Due to Identical FAR Profile appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT ruled that margins agreed under a Bilateral Advance Pricing Agreement (BAPA) can be applied to non-US Associated Enterprise (AE) transactions if their Functional, Asset, and Risk (FAR) profiles are identical. This decision emphasizes consistency in transfer pricing application and was made in the absence of separate benchmarking by the TPO.

title: Customs Valuation Difference Alone Cannot Prove Undisclosed Purchases: ITAT Mumbai
summary: Mumbai ITAT observed that assessable value under customs law may differ from invoice value and such variation by itself cannot justify addition under Section 69C without further evidence. The post Customs Valuation Difference Alone Cannot Prove Undisclosed Purchases: ITAT Mumbai appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Mumbai ruled that a mere difference between assessable value under customs law and invoice value does not, by itself, justify an addition under Section 69C for undisclosed purchases. The Tribunal stated that further evidence is required to prove such undisclosed purchases.

title: Chambers Practice Law Guide – USA Transfer Pricing 2026 - White & Case LLP
summary: Chambers Practice Law Guide – USA Transfer Pricing 2026 White & Case LLP
ai_summary: This White & Case LLP publication serves as a comprehensive practitioner's guide to US transfer pricing law for 2026. It provides an in-depth analysis of current transfer pricing regulations, methodologies, and compliance practices specific to the United States.

title: VAT and transfer pricing: beyond the arm’s-length principle - International Tax Review
summary: VAT and transfer pricing: beyond the arm’s-length principle International Tax Review
ai_summary: This International Tax Review article explores the complex intersection of VAT and transfer pricing, specifically addressing instances where their application might extend beyond the traditional arm's-length principle. It analyzes the challenges and potential divergences in applying both regimes to intercompany transactions.

title: Accendra Pays $19M To Settle IRS Transfer Pricing Matter - Law360
summary: Accendra Pays $19M To Settle IRS Transfer Pricing Matter Law360
ai_summary: Accendra has agreed to pay $19 million to settle a transfer pricing dispute with the Internal Revenue Service (IRS). This significant settlement resolves a long-standing transfer pricing matter between the company and the US tax authority.

title: When Transfer Pricing Documentation Is Required in Vietnam - Vietnam Briefing
summary: When Transfer Pricing Documentation Is Required in Vietnam Vietnam Briefing
ai_summary: This Vietnam Briefing article clarifies the specific requirements for transfer pricing documentation in Vietnam. It outlines the conditions under which local entities are mandated to prepare and submit transfer pricing reports to the Vietnamese tax authorities.

title: EU Court Sides with Stellantis in Key VAT Transfer Pricing Case
summary: EU Court Sides with Stellantis in Key VAT Transfer Pricing Case news.bloombergtax.com
ai_summary: The EU Court has delivered a significant ruling in favor of Stellantis in a key case concerning the interplay between VAT and transfer pricing principles. This decision provides important legal precedent on how VAT rules should align with transfer pricing adjustments for multinational corporations within the European Union.

title: Q&A: Navigating transfer pricing disputes - Financier Worldwide
summary: Q&A: Navigating transfer pricing disputes Financier Worldwide
ai_summary: This Financier Worldwide Q&A provides expert insights and strategies for navigating complex transfer pricing disputes. It offers practical guidance for multinational corporations facing audits, controversies, and potential litigation related to their intercompany transactions.

title: STS launched the Transfer Price Information form
summary: STS launched the Transfer Price Information form logos-pres.md
ai_summary: The STS (likely State Tax Service) in Moldova has officially launched a new Transfer Price Information form for taxpayers. This update introduces a new procedural requirement for reporting intercompany transaction data to the Moldovan tax authorities.

title: ITAT Orders Exclusion of Comparable as DRP Had Already Held It Functionally Different
summary: Delhi ITAT directed exclusion of a comparable company engaged in video conferencing solutions after noting that the DRP had already found it functionally incomparable to the assessee’s software distribution business. The post ITAT Orders Exclusion of Comparable as DRP Had Already Held It Functionally Different appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Delhi Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) has directed the exclusion of a comparable company in a transfer pricing assessment, ruling that the Dispute Resolution Panel (DRP) had already determined it to be functionally different. The decision underscores the critical importance of functional comparability in selecting benchmarks for software distribution businesses in India.

title: Advertisement Expenses Cannot Trigger TP Adjustment Without AE Arrangement
summary: The Delhi ITAT held that advertisement and marketing expenses could not be treated as an international transaction without evidence of an arrangement with the associated enterprise. The Tribunal deleted the transfer pricing adjustment and rejected the application of the Bright Line Test. The post Advertisement Expenses Cannot Trigger TP Adjustment Without AE Arrangement appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Delhi ITAT has held that advertisement and marketing expenses cannot trigger a transfer pricing adjustment without clear evidence of an underlying international transaction or arrangement with an associated enterprise. The Tribunal also explicitly rejected the application of the Bright Line Test in this specific Indian case, deleting the proposed adjustment.

title: Diversified Publishing and Platform Business Makes Company Functionally Incomparable to Captive ITES Provider: ITAT Deletes ₹18.80 Cr TP Adjustment [Read Order] - Taxscan
summary: Diversified Publishing and Platform Business Makes Company Functionally Incomparable to Captive ITES Provider: ITAT Deletes ₹18.80 Cr TP Adjustment [Read Order] Taxscan
ai_summary: The Indian Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) deleted a ₹18.80 Cr transfer pricing adjustment, ruling that a diversified publishing and platform business was functionally incomparable to a captive ITES provider. This decision emphasizes the importance of accurate functional analysis and comparability in transfer pricing disputes in India.

title: State and Local Tax Collections Per Capita by State, 2026
summary: Tax collections vary widely by state, making per capita collections figures—a measure of collections per person—especially useful, as they allow comparisons across differences in tax rates and bases, economic capacities, and policy decisions that impact the size and scope of government.
ai_summary: This article examines state and local tax collections per capita across different US states for 2026. It highlights how varying tax rates and economic capacities impact tax revenue figures and allow for comparisons of policy decisions.

title: CPC Can’t Ignore Finance Act Benefit Just Because ITR Had No Column – Pune ITAT Allows 25% Corporate Tax Rate Claim!
summary: The Tribunal ruled that CPC’s application of a 30% tax rate merely due to absence of turnover disclosure in the return form could not override the concessional rate provided by law. The post CPC Can’t Ignore Finance Act Benefit Just Because ITR Had No Column – Pune ITAT Allows 25% Corporate Tax Rate Claim! appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Pune ITAT ruled that the Central Processing Centre (CPC) cannot ignore a taxpayer's claim for a concessional 25% corporate tax rate, even if the Income Tax Return form lacked a specific column for turnover disclosure. This decision clarifies that substantive law overrides procedural deficiencies in Indian tax filings.

title: Digital Companies Earn From India Without Fair Tax Liability
summary: The article explains how multinational digital companies generate substantial revenue from Indian users while shifting profits to low-tax jurisdictions. Existing transfer pricing rules struggle to capture value created through digital presence and data monetisation. The post Digital Companies Earn From India Without Fair Tax Liability appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: This article discusses how multinational digital companies generate significant revenue from Indian users while employing profit shifting strategies to low-tax jurisdictions. It highlights the challenges existing transfer pricing rules face in accurately attributing value derived from digital presence and data monetization in India.

title: No TDS on Bandwidth Payments as IPLC Charges Are Not Royalty: Madras HC
summary: The Court rejected the Revenue’s argument that payments for bandwidth and telecom connectivity constituted consideration for use of equipment. It held that the remittances were not royalty and therefore outside the scope of TDS liability. The post No TDS on Bandwidth Payments as IPLC Charges Are Not Royalty: Madras HC appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Madras High Court ruled that payments for bandwidth and International Private Leased Circuit (IPLC) charges do not constitute royalty and are therefore not subject to Tax Deducted at Source (TDS) in India. The court rejected the Revenue's argument that these payments were for the use of equipment, clarifying the definition of royalty for telecom services.
